title: phucagrostis manatorum
side_effects: Phucagrostis manatorum can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing severe allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and swelling. Prolonged use of the plant has also been linked to gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In some cases, improper use of Phucagrostis manatorum can cause cardiovascular problems, including rapid heartbeat, high blood pressure, and in severe cases, heart failure. Additionally, the plant may interact with other medications, exacerbating existing health conditions or causing new ones to develop.
